title: Data Enrichment - Turning Domain Names into Data

questions.question: What is the primary risk of sending emails to an unverified list?

options.text: The emails might be too long.
High bounce rates will damage your domain reputation, causing future emails to go to spam.
You might annoy verified people.
It costs too much money.

questions.explanation: Domain reputation is fragile. Bouncing >5% of emails signals verification algorithms that you are a spammer.

questions.question: What is a 'Waterfall' enrichment strategy?

options.text: Sending emails while standing under a waterfall.
Using multiple data providers sequentially (if A fails, try B, then C) to maximize coverage.
Enriching all data at once with the most expensive provider.
Washing your data with soap.

questions.explanation: No single provider has 100% data. Waterfalling allows you to cascade through providers to fill in the gaps.

questions.question: How should you handle 'Catch-All' (Accept All) email statuses?

options.text: Treat them as Valid and send immediately.
Delete them all.
Treat them as Risky. Only send to high-value (Tier 1) accounts, ideally from a burner domain, or verify with specialized tools.
Call them instead.

questions.explanation: Catch-Alls are risky because they don't bounce immediately but can still damage reputation. Proceed with caution.

questions.question: What is 'Contextual Enrichment' (e.g., using Clay)?

options.text: Finding the prospect's home address.
Scraping the web for specific data points (e.g., 'Do they use Shopify?', 'Recent LinkedIn Post') to enable personalization.
Adding colorful fonts to your spreadsheet.
Checking if the email is valid.

questions.explanation: Contextual enrichment goes beyond contact info. It gathers intelligence to make your outreach relevant.

questions.question: Why should you avoid buying huge static lists (annual contracts)?

options.text: They are too heavy to download.
Data decays. People change jobs constantly, so old lists become inaccurate quickly.
They are illegal.
Spreadsheets confuse computers.

questions.explanation: B2B data has a high decay rate (~30%/year). Buying credits 'as you go' ensures you get fresh data.

questions.question: What data enrichment sources would you use to turn a basic company name into a full prospect profile? List at least 3 sources and what data they provide.
questions.promptForAI: Assess data enrichment strategy. Check for: (1) variety of sources, (2) practical applicability, (3) data quality considerations. Suggest additional enrichment tools or techniques.
